model.label: Gemini 3 Flash
model.description: Google • Fast Gemini model optimized for low-latency reasoning, multimodal inputs, and tool use.

pick.rationale: TCU's defensive identity and performance in the Big 12 provide a more reliable path to victory in a neutral-site tournament setting. Although Ohio State enters with momentum from a strong finish to the Big Ten season, the Horned Frogs' ability to generate stops and their experience against high-level offenses should allow them to edge out the Buckeyes in a typical 8/9 defensive battle. The Horned Frogs' top-30 defensive efficiency is the deciding factor in what is expected to be a very close matchup.

reasoningStep.title: TCU's Defensive Edge vs Ohio State's Momentum
reasoningStep.summary: TCU's top-30 defense and Big 12 battle-tested record provide a slight advantage over a surging Ohio State team.
evidence: TCU (22-11) ranks 30th in adjusted defensive efficiency (Torvik) and 43rd overall (KenPom).
Ohio State (21-12) is making its first tournament appearance in four years after a late-season surge.
TCU finished 11-7 in the highly competitive Big 12 conference, demonstrating consistency against elite competition.

toolCalls.summary: Fetched https://www.usatoday.com/story/sports/college/basketball/2026/03/15/ohio-state-tcu-march-madness-prediction-ncaa-bracket/89025454007/ and extracted 3000 characters of text.

result.text: Ohio State vs TCU prediction, pick, odds March Madness first round Skip to main content Home Olympics NFL NCAAB NCAAW NBA Women's Sports NCAAF MLB WNBA Sports Betting Big Ten Sports SEC Sports Boxing Coaches Salaries Draft Hub Fantasy Football For The Win Formula 1 Golfweek High School Sports High School Sports Awards IndyCar MMA NASCAR National Signing Day NHL Soccer Sports Business Sports Pulse Sports Seriously Tennis WWE Youth Sports Outdoors Fantasy Bet With the People Clear Play Arizona Sports Indiana Sports Michigan Sports Ohio Sports Tennessee Sports Wisconsin Teams For Subscribers Ohio State basketball vs TCU prediction: Our pick for 2026 NCAA bracket first round Adam Jardy Columbus Dispatch Ohio State is dancing for the first time in four years. The Buckeyes have earned a No. 8 seed in the 2026 men's NCAA Tournament and a first-round date with No. 9 seed TCU at 12:15 p.m. March 19 in Greenville, South Carolina. Ohio State closed the season with four consecutive wins before falling in the quarterfinal round of the Big Ten Tournament in Chicago to bring a 21-12 overall record into March Madness. TCU is 22-11 overall and went 11-7 in the Big 12 Conference. Here are four predictions for this first-round matchup between Ohio State and TCU in Bon Secours Wellness Arena: TCU's defense will test Ohio State's offense The Buckeyes are enjoying one of their most prolific offensive seasons in program history. Ohio State is No. 17 nationally in adjusted offensive efficiency according to KenPom.com, averaging 124.3 points per 100 possessions. TCU is No. 22 in adjusted defensive efficiency, allowing 97.8 points per 100 possessions. The Horned Frogs are 31st nationally in defensive turnover rate, turning teams over on 19.7% of their possessions. Ohio State is 100th nationally in offensive turnover rate, committing turnovers on 15.5% of their possessions. Think you can beat our expert? Join USA TODAY’s Bracket Challenge today! Ready to win March Madness? Join USA TODAY’s Survivor Pool today! John Mobley Jr.'s 3-point shooting could be key The Horned Frogs don't take a lot of 3-pointers and rank No. 233 nationally in percentage of shots taken from beyond the arc. They make only 33.1% of their 3s, which ranks 223rd nationally. Ohio State sophomore guard John Mobley Jr. is 94 for 225 (41.8%) from 3 and senior Bruce Thornton is 60 for 150 (40.0%). TCU's best 3-point shooter is Jayden Pierre, who is 51 for 141 (36.2%) from deep. TCU is led by undersized bigs Xavier Edmonds and David Punch TCU's highest-rated offensive players at KenPom are freshman Xavier Edmonds, a 6-foot-8, 245-pound forward, and David Punch, a 6-7, 246-pound forward. Punch averages 14.3 points and 6.7 rebounds while Edmonds averages 12.6 and 6.6, respectively. No player in TCU's primary rotation is taller than Punch, while Ohio State starts three players at 6-6 or taller. Buy Ohio State NCAA tournament tickets vs. TCU Bruce Thornton's size advantage should help Ohio State backcourt The Buc

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.

John Mobley Jr.'s 3-point shooting could be key The Horned Frogs don't take a lot of 3-pointers and rank No. 233 nationally in percentage of shots taken from beyond the arc. They make only 33.1% of their 3s, which ranks 223rd nationally. Ohio State sophomore guard John Mobley Jr. is 94 for 225 (41.8%) from 3 and senior Bruce Thornton is 60 for 150 (40.0%). TCU's best 3-point shooter is Jayden Pierre, who is 51 for 141 (36.2%) from deep. TCU is led by undersized bigs Xavier Edmonds and David Punch TCU's highest-rated offensive players at KenPom are freshman Xavier Edmonds, a 6-foot-8, 245-pound forward, and David Punch, a 6-7, 246-pound forward. Punch averages 14.3 points and 6.7 rebounds while Edmonds averages 12.6 and 6.6, respectively. No player in TCU's primary rotation is taller than Punch, while Ohio State starts three players at 6-6 or taller.
